key_points: Area is the measure of the surface covered by a two-dimensional shape
Area is measured in square units such as cm² or m²
A rectangle’s area depends on its length and width
A square is a special rectangle with all sides equal
Triangles, parallelograms, rhombuses and trapeziums have specific formulas to calculate their area
examples: Area of rectangle (10 cm × 5 cm) = 50 cm²
Area of triangle with base 8 cm and height 5 cm = 20 cm²
Area of parallelogram with base 7 cm and height 6 cm = 42 cm²
formulas: Rhombus → Area = ½ × product of diagonals
Trapezium → Area = ½ × height × (sum of parallel sides)
exam_tips: Always use square units when writing the final answer
Identify the correct base and perpendicular height
Use the correct formula for each geometric shape
Draw diagrams to understand shapes clearly
